title: Features and preprocessing

publication.changeSummary: Source-linked AI Foundations draft for Features and preprocessing; all claims, examples, metrics, code, safety boundaries, and answer oracles require independent human review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: scikit-learn User Guide; 1.9.x target, accessed 2026-07; User Guide sections 3 and 7: model selection, pipelines, preprocessing, imputation, and leakage-safe evaluation; atomic topic: Features and preprocessing
sources.claim: Feature preprocessing converts raw fields into meaningful numeric representations, with learned imputation, scaling, encoding, and selection fitted only on training data and replayed unchanged on later data.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Feature preprocessing converts raw fields into meaningful numeric representations, with learned imputation, scaling, encoding, and selection fitted only on training data and replayed unchanged on later data.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A ColumnTransformer imputes/scales numeric columns and one-hot encodes categories inside a pipeline fitted during cross-validation folds. Your goal is to build and explain a preprocessing pipeline that handles numeric and categorical inputs. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: python quality project; Problem and environment characteristics; Sampling, labels and data quality; python collections dict set. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Bounded operational model
sections.body: Feature preprocessing converts raw fields into meaningful numeric representations, with learned imputation, scaling, encoding, and selection fitted only on training data and replayed unchanged on later data. Any capability or quality claim remains limited to an explicit task, population, conditions, baseline, and recorded evidence.

sections.heading: Failure boundary and non-claim
sections.body: Scaling needs vary by model, categories can be unseen, timestamps can leak future information, high-cardinality encoding can memorize, and a pipeline prevents procedural leakage but not a conceptually leaked feature. The learner must reject this overclaim: It is safe to fit a scaler or category vocabulary on the full dataset because preprocessing does not use the target directly.

includes: Encode, scale or transform only using training-derived information and meaningful representations.
Build and explain a preprocessing pipeline that handles numeric and categorical inputs.
excludes: Scaling needs vary by model, categories can be unseen, timestamps can leak future information, high-cardinality encoding can memorize, and a pipeline prevents procedural leakage but not a conceptually leaked feature.

outcomes: Build and explain a preprocessing pipeline that handles numeric and categorical inputs.

examples.title: Features and preprocessing: bounded direct case
examples.situation: A ColumnTransformer imputes/scales numeric columns and one-hot encodes categories inside a pipeline fitted during cross-validation folds.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the exact data, state, metric, or tool behavior: A ColumnTransformer imputes/scales numeric columns and one-hot encodes categories inside a pipeline fitted during cross-validation folds.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the claim boundary with: Fit every data-derived preprocessing step on the training partition or fold only, and exclude features unavailable at the real prediction time.
examples.result: This example supports only the atomic outcome because Fit every data-derived preprocessing step on the training partition or fold only, and exclude features unavailable at the real prediction time.

examples.title: Features and preprocessing: stress and transfer case
examples.situation: A feature containing final resolution time is removed because no pipeline can make information unavailable at prediction time legitimate.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name the changed population, input, assumption, or failure: A feature containing final resolution time is removed because no pipeline can make information unavailable at prediction time legitimate.

Step 2 — Keep the non-claim visible: Scaling needs vary by model, categories can be unseen, timestamps can leak future information, high-cardinality encoding can memorize, and a pipeline prevents procedural leakage but not a conceptually leaked feature.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting overclaim: It is safe to fit a scaler or category vocabulary on the full dataset because preprocessing does not use the target directly.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Build and explain a preprocessing pipeline that handles numeric and categorical inputs.

misconceptions.correction: Fit every data-derived preprocessing step on the training partition or fold only, and exclude features unavailable at the real prediction time.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Identify the first unsupported inference in this belief and the evidence required to narrow it: It is safe to fit a scaler or category vocabulary on the full dataset because preprocessing does not use the target directly.

analogy.example: a provenance-controlled laboratory that defines the decision, inspects samples and labels, derives transformations, and seals evaluation partitions can introduce Features and preprocessing only after its canonical technical definition and evidence boundary are stated.
usefulFor: problem framing, data quality, preprocessing, and leakage control
limitations: clean procedures cannot recover absent populations, authorize data, or make a harmful target appropriate

Keep this boundary in view: Scaling needs vary by model, categories can be unseen, timestamps can leak future information, high-cardinality encoding can memorize, and a pipeline prevents procedural leakage but not a conceptually leaked feature.
